name: Sigyn
description: Sigyn is a goddess in Norse mythology, primarily known for her unwavering loyalty and devotion to her husband, Loki. Her origins are somewhat obscure, and she is not prominently featured in many surviving Norse texts. However, her role in Loki's punishment is significant. After Loki was caught and punished for his role in Baldr's death, he was bound to a rock with the entrails of his son Narfi. Skadi placed a venomous serpent above him, dripping poison onto his face. Sigyn, ever faithful, sits beside him, holding a bowl to catch the venom. When the bowl fills, she must leave to empty it, and during that time, the poison drips onto Loki, causing him to writhe in agony, which is said to cause earthquakes. Sigyn's endurance and compassion in the face of such suffering are the defining characteristics of her persona. While she doesn't possess overt powers in the traditional sense, her strength lies in her unwavering dedication and ability to endure hardship. Her role is a testament to loyalty and love, even in the most dire circumstances. Some interpretations suggest she embodies the enduring power of love and compassion in a world often defined by conflict and chaos. She is a silent but powerful figure, demonstrating the strength found in quiet devotion.
subtitle: Loyal wife of Loki, enduring compassion

keyMyths.title: Loki's Punishment
keyMyths.description: Sigyn remains by Loki's side as he is punished, catching the venom of a serpent to ease his suffering. Her loyalty is tested as she periodically empties the bowl.

keyMyths.title: Sigyn's Devotion
keyMyths.description: Sigyn's unwavering devotion is a central theme, showcasing her commitment despite Loki's actions. She represents enduring love and compassion.

keyMyths.title: Origins Obscured
keyMyths.description: The origins and parentage of Sigyn are not explicitly stated in surviving texts, leaving her background largely mysterious. This lack of information adds to her enigmatic nature.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
